evals.prompt: When should I use WebSockets vs Server-Sent Events in NestJS?
evals.expected_output: WebSockets for bi-directional (chat, games, collaborative editing). SSE for uni-directional (notifications, live feeds, log streaming). SSE is simpler: standard HTTP, no sticky sessions needed.

assertions.description: Distinguishes WebSocket vs SSE use cases

evals.prompt: How do I scale NestJS WebSockets across multiple Kubernetes pods?
evals.expected_output: Use Redis Adapter (@socket.io/redis-adapter). Each pod subscribes via Redis. Without it, client on Pod A won't receive events emitted by Pod B.

evals.prompt: How do I authenticate WebSocket connections in NestJS?
evals.expected_output: Validate JWT in the handleConnection() lifecycle method. HTTP Guards do not trigger on WS connections efficiently. Disconnect immediately if JWT is invalid.

assertions.description: Warns that HTTP Guards don't work for WS

should_not_trigger: How do I implement SSE in Go?
How do I configure NestJS database?
How do I set up WebSocket in Spring Boot?
